description_long: This is a compact, single-story residential house with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in reddish-brown tiles. The exterior walls are finished in a light gray plaster above a stone-clad foundation, giving the structure a sturdy and traditional appearance. The roof features a prominent brick chimney and several skylights, providing both ventilation and natural light to the attic or upper space. The front facade includes a small set of concrete steps leading to a recessed entryway, flanked by two rectangular windows. The windows are symmetrically arranged, and the house has minimal decorative elements, emphasizing functionality. The overall design suggests a practical suburban or rural dwelling, typical of Central or Eastern European neighborhoods.
description: A single-story house with a steep red-tiled gable roof, stone foundation, and symmetrical windows. The front features a recessed entryway with steps and a brick chimney on the roof.
description_view_0: End view showing the gable roof, two lower windows, and a small attic window above.
description_view_1: Front view with the main entryway, steps, two windows, and the chimney visible on the roof.
description_view_2: Rear gable end with no windows, showing the stone foundation and plain wall.
description_view_3: Opposite front view, similar to view 1 but with the entryway and steps on the right side.
attribute_entryway_design: Recessed entry with concrete steps, no porch, and minimal decorative elements
